element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Cosmetic differences only; choice has no mechanical impact
Basic racing without tier progression pressure
Entry fees create risk-versus-reward decisions between upgrading and advancing
Prize money enables incremental customization; players must balance upgrade investment against tier advancement fees
Limited-use tactical items for multiplayer and AI opponent interaction
Creative sandbox mode; allows custom course design from simple ovals to complex hills and curves
Split-screen mandatory display; second screen shows opponent view simultaneously

typical_relief_triggers: Completing a race and earning prize money
Advancing to next tier (after paying entry fee)
typical_tension_triggers: Entry fee requirement to advance tier (risk-versus-reward choice)
Insufficient prize money forcing decision between upgrade and tier advancement
AI opponent competition with split-screen view creating awareness pressure
Limited-use consumable items requiring tactical deployment
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progression-gated racing loop with economic tension between vehicle improvement and tier advancement; cyclical risk-reward decision-making drives career mode pacing

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Track editor allows custom course complexity variation (straight ovals vs. complex hills/curves); player-designed tracks signal optional complexity exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Entry-level races with modest entry fees; allows capital accumulation for initial upgrades
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Higher tier races with increased entry fees; forces strategic decisions between vehicle improvement and progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Significant advancement fees create higher stakes; requires optimized upgrade decisions and skilled AI opponent management
notable_spikes: Mid-career tier jumps—entry fees scale upward against fixed prize money
Late-career AI competitiveness—opponents become challenging with improved vehicle configurations

notable_level_patterns.description: Racing career structured in tiers with escalating entry fees. Players earn prize money per race and must decide whether to upgrade vehicles or save capital for tier advancement. Economic pressure creates strategic decision points.
Even in single-player career mode, split-screen display shows player view and AI opponent view simultaneously. Creates spatial awareness of competitive position without pausing or screen transition.
Limited-use offensive items (oil slicks, mines) available for purchase with prize money. Creates secondary tactical layer beyond raw racing skill and vehicle upgrades.

Inherited from Racing Destruction Set, allows players to design and save custom courses ranging from simple ovals to complex terrain with hills and curves. Decouples level design progression from career mode.
